title: 2.1 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lesson 2.1.
topic_path: History of the Universe and Earth

assessment_items.question_content:
**When did our Solar System form, approximately?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: We do not know when our solar system was formed.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The first four thresholds cover the creation of our Universe and its celestial bodies. What is the order of these thresholds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Big Bang, Stars Light Up, New Chemical Elements, Earth & The Solar System
New Chemical Elements, The Big Bang, Stars Light Up, Earth
The Big Bang, New Chemical Elements, Stars Light Up, Earth & The Solar System
The Big Bang, New Chemical Elements, Stars Light Up, Life

assessment_items.question_content:
**Heavy chemical elements like iron, gold, and platinum are formed in the cores of dying stars. How then did these elements end up across our Universe, including on Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Some stars explode as supernovae while others collide like neutron stars, sending elements flying.
All stars explode upon their death, sending elements flying.
Stars have existed in every space of the Universe at one time or another, leaving behind elements.
These elements are pulled together by gravity to form planets.
Gravity pulls together clouds of atoms, causing pressure and temperature to rise until atoms fuse together.
Atoms are attracted to similar atoms by electromagnetism, increasing pressure until temperatures rise.
Gravity pulls clouds of heavy chemical elements like carbon until they fuse together.
Dying stars collapse into themselves, forming a new star from the explosion.
